message:
refactor(acl): extract hash helpers + persist sanitize-payload + upstream-shape ACL errors (frankenredis-npil)

Follow-up polish to the SHA-256 ACL hashing landed in 27947a4. Three
concerns get tightened so our ACL output matches upstream Redis byte-for-
byte and the code stops repeating the hash primitives at every callsite.

crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:

1. Shared helpers at the top of the module:

     fn sha256_hex_bytes(input: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8>
     fn normalize_acl_hash(hash: &str) -> Option<Vec<u8>>

   sha256_hex_bytes replaces three open-coded Sha256::new / update /
   finalize / hex::encode sequences in AclUser::check_password,
   AuthState::apply_requirepass, and AuthState::add_user — those had
   drifted slightly from each other (some `use sha2::Digest` imports
   were inline, one was module-level). normalize_acl_hash captures the
   "64 lowercase hex digits" validation for `#<hash>` / `!<hash>` once
   instead of repeating the char-class check and the case-fold in both
   arms of set_user.

2. apply_requirepass + add_user now hash passwords on entry. Previously
   `requirepass <plaintext>` and the internal add_user(username,
   plaintext) test hook stored raw bytes, which broke parity with the
   `>password` modifier that did hash. All three entry points now
   converge on hashed storage so AUTH works regardless of which path
   installed the credential.

3. ACL flags now include "sanitize-payload" in the parts list, which
   Redis persists in both ACL SAVE output and reports back in GETUSER.
   Accepting this marker during set_user (eq_ignore_ascii_case no-op)
   lets ACL LOAD round-trip without spurious "syntax error" rejections
   on saved ACL files from a real Redis instance. Our implementation
   always behaves as if sanitize-payload is on, so parsing is a no-op.

4. Password serialization in GETUSER/describe-user switched from
   ">plaintext" to "#<hash>" since the stored form is already the hex
   hash — printing ">..." would leak a "fake plaintext" that is really
   a hex digest, which is confusing and breaks round-trip through
   ACL LOAD.

5. Runtime GETUSER response no longer re-hex-encodes passwords (they
   were already hex strings); dropped the redundant
   RespFrame::BulkString(Some(hex::encode(p).into_bytes())) that was
   double-encoding.

6. Error messages for malformed #/! modifiers switched from our custom
   "Hash must be exactly 64 hex characters" to upstream Redis's
   "Syntax error" wording so clients that branch on ACL SETUSER error
   strings don't diverge.

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_acl.json +
crates/fr-conformance/tests/smoke.rs: fixture updates and a smoke case
that exercises the new GETUSER shape, proving the sanitize-payload flag,
SHA-256 hex storage, and "#<hash>" serialization all line up with the
live differential oracle. New test
acl_getuser_reports_sha256_passwords_for_new_restricted_users asserts
GETUSER(alice) after SETUSER alice on >password123 returns the exact
sha256 hex of "password123" and the sanitize-payload flag.
